title: graphroot and rootless_storage_path are ignored in global storage.conf
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

Somewhat similar to #7513... I am unable to set the graphroot (or rootless_storage_path) in the global storage.conf file.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. rm ~/.config/containers/storage.conf

2. Edit /etc/containers/storage.conf to set graphroot = "/scratch/$USER/containers/storage" (or anything really)

3. Run "podman info | grep scratch"

**Describe the results you received:**

Empty output.

**Describe the results you expected:**

  graphRoot: /scratch/ploprest/containers/storage
  volumePath: /scratch/ploprest/containers/storage/volumes

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15
Built:        Sun Sep 27 19:07:17 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Can you try this on a brand new user account?  Or you can do 
```
$ podman system reset
$ podman system migrate 
$ podman info 
```
Does it change?
@rhatdan Same result. Are you unable to reproduce?
Have not tried yet.  I attempted to fix this earlier, but maybe I missed something, (Most likely)

@rhatdan I confirm this issue: bug #7546 is not fixed.
Updated today to version 2.1.1, but if I remove `~/.config/containers/storage.conf` I get:
```
$ podman info | fgrep graphRoot
  graphRoot: /home/rcastagnola/.local/share/containers/storage
```
while `/etc/containers/storage.conf` contains:
```
$ grep ^[^#] /etc/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/mnt/containers/storage"
rootless_storage_path = "/mnt/containers/storage-$UID"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
]
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on"
[storage.options.thinpool]

```

@rhatdan Thanks.

Incidentally, #7879 is more of a showstopper for me... This bug has a work-around; i.e. I can ask each user to edit .config/containers/storage.conf. #7879 has no work-around at all, and it renders additionalimagestores unusable.

My understanding from  looking at the code is that the `/etc/containers/storage.conf` file is never read when run in rootless mode, so no way to get it to work.
What I would like to understand is what is the wanted behaviour: should `/etc/containers/storage.conf` file be used to configure the storage for rootless mode as well, or should it be kept separated?
In the latter case it doesn't make sense to me to have a different config name to override the default value: `graphroot` could be used instead of `rootless_storage_path`

Sorry for my last comment, I was wrong: I totally missed that the file is parsed into init function.

Anyway, adding few Printf commands I found out where the issue is.
Into the function `defaultStoreOptionsIsolated` (utils.go), the variable err before the last if block (`if rootless && rootlessUID != 0`) contains:
`err: stat /home/roberto/.config/containers/storage.conf: no such file or directory`
because the user conf is missing, thus the block is bypassed and the RootlessStoragePath value is not used to change GraphRoot value.

I have tryed then to create the file `~/.config/containers/storage.conf` without redefining the property `rootless_storage_path`, but in this case the variable `storageOpts.RootlessStoragePath` became empty because of the code:
```
storageOpts = StoreOptions{}
reloadConfigurationFileIfNeeded(storageConf, &storageOpts)
```
thus creating the user conf file the RootlessStoragePath value is forgotten.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This is fixed in the master branch.
